title: carex stenophylla subsp. rigescens
intro: Carex stenophylla subsp. rigescens, also known as the stiff-leaved sedge, is a sub-species of the Carex stenophylla plant, belonging to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial grass-like plant native to certain regions, valued for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. Some herbalists also recommend the plant for its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm these uses.
